rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees are placed in valid scopes according to the component type or implementation context
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in assumptions and guarantees
Write output dependency contracts that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
